note: Stage P3 Track J2 triage (DEV-085) -- all 203 ambiguous-direction pairs resolved. Methodology: (1) majority-vote when one direction has strictly more corroborating rows (123 entries); (2) spine-priority tie-break (apollodorus-bibliotheca > hesiod-theogony > homer-iliad), matching canonical_edge.py's own _pick_winner logic, for ties where one direction's best source outranks the other's (32 entries); (3) manual source-verification against the actual corpus text for the remaining genuine ties (48 entries). One entry (Eumelus/Pheres) rejected outright -- a generation-skip error (Pheres is Eumelus's grandfather via Admetus, not his direct parent). Two entities discovered along the way needed adding: `Phocus` (Aeacus/Psamathe's son, killed by Peleus/Telamon -- extracted throughout as the unrelated 'Phorcus', a primordial sea-god; fixed here and in 3 pre-existing main-file rows) and `Creon (father of Lycomedes)` (a 4th distinct Creon, a minor Iliad-only patronymic unrelated to the Thebes/Corinth/Hercules Creons already disambiguated in DEV-081). One entry (Zeus/Hellen) required a 3-way split matching DEV-082's Helen/Hellen/Helle precedent -- most of its rows were actually about Helen of Troy, mislabeled, plus one legitimate contested alt-parentage claim for the real (male) Hellen.

decisions.reason: Zeus/Hellen conflates male Hellen (contested alt-parentage per [1.7.2]) and Helen of Troy (Zeus/Leda, well-established); split by passage_ref, dropped the 1 reversed row.
Eumelus is Admetus's son, not Pheres's direct son (Pheres is Admetus's father, i.e. Eumelus's grandfather) -- a generation-skip error; neither direction is a correct parent_of claim.
